to Google just completely broke the rules of design. Like actually broke them. You used to need Figma, a designer, hours of back and forth, endless revisions, a whole process just to get one screen looking right. Now you just open a tool, describe what you want and watch it build in front of your eyes. No design skills, no coding, no team, no experience needed at all. And the new update, you can literally talk to it out loud and it builds your UI in real time as you speak. voice input, infinite canvas, an AI that actually remembers everything you told it and keeps your brand consistent across every single screen. I'm not exaggerating. This changes how fast you can go from idea to product. And I mean dramatically faster. If you're building anything online right now, an agency, a community, a SAS, a landing page, literally anything, this is the update you need to understand because the people who figure this out first are going to move faster than everyone around them. And I'm going to break down every single part of it so you can actually use it today. Let's go. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian reads every single comment. So, drop something below and he will see it. Okay, so Google Stitch just dropped a huge update. And before I get into it, let me give you the quick version of what Google Stitch actually is because if you haven't heard of it yet, you are going to want to know this right now. Google Stitch is an AI powered design tool from Google Labs. You give it a text prompt or upload a rough sketch and it builds you a full UI, real layout, real design, actual working front-end code in HTML, CSS, and React ready to use straight away. You don't need to know how to code. You don't need Figma skills. You don't need design experience. You just need to know what you want and be able to describe it. That's it. That alone was already impressive. But this new update took everything to a completely different level. Five major upgrades. And by the end of this video, you'll know exactly how each one works and how you can use them to build faster and smarter than you ever have before. The first big update is the infinite canvas. Before this design tools worked screen by screen, one artboard, then the next, then the next. It was slow, disconnected, and you could never see the full picture at once. You'd finish one screen, move to the next, and completely lose the context of how everything fit together. Now, with Stitch, the canvas is unlimited. There are no artboard limits, no borders, no boundaries, nothing stopping you from spreading your ideas out as wide as you need. Think of a whiteboard that never ends. You can lay out your entire product in one single space. Every screen, every user flow, every journey, all connected, all visible, all in one place at the same time. For something like the AI profit boardroom, this is a total gamecher. You could map out the whole member experience in one canvas. The landing page, the onboarding flow, the member dashboard, the content feed, the community pages, all of it connected, visible, flowing together naturally. No more jumping between files. No more losing track of where things live or how screens connect to each other. It turns design from a screen by screen grind into a real thinking tool. You can see the whole story at once and make better decisions because of it. Update two is the smarter design agent. And this one fixes the biggest problem that has existed in AI design tools since day one. Here's what used to happen. You'd build something. It looked great. You were happy with it. Then you'd ask it to change one small thing, something tiny, and it would completely forget everything it just did. Different fonts, different spacing, different colors, a totally different vibe, completely inconsistent output every single time. It was genuinely frustrating. It broke your momentum. It created extra work. You'd spend more time fixing AI mistakes than you saved by using the tool in the first place. Google fixed that with this update. The new design agent remembers your full project history, your colors, your style, your brand guidelines, your previous prompts, your decisions, every single iteration builds properly on the last one without losing what came before. So, if you're building a landing page for the AI profit boardroom, a page that explains AI automation, shows the real value of the community and is designed to bring in new members and you say make it feel more minimal. It doesn't forget the dark theme and bold typography you already established. It just refineses, it improves, it stays consistent with everything you already built. That is a massive deal. Consistency is what separates AI design that looks professional from AI design that looks like a mess. And now that consistency is baked in automatically because if you're watching this video, you're someone who actually wants to use AI to build and grow things, not just watch videos about it, actually use it in a real way. That's exactly what the AI profit boardroom is built for. We take tools like Google Stitch and break down precisely how to plug them into a real workflow. How to move faster, how to produce better output, how to use AI every day without burning out or getting lost in the noise. We show you the practical side, the stuff that actually works, not theory, not hype. real use cases and real strategies for real builders. If that sounds like you, the link to join is in the description below. Come check it out. Okay, back to Stitch. This is the update that genuinely surprised me, and I think it's going to surprise you, too. Voice mode. You can now speak to Google Stitch out loud, and it responds by building your UI in real time as you talk. Say, make this section more minimal. Done. You say, add a pricing table below the hero section. Done. You say, "Design a landing page for the AI profit boardroom that communicates the value of AI automation clearly, explains why people should join the community and feels premium and high converting and it builds it a real landing page for a real product based purely on what you just said out loud in plain English. Google is calling this concept vibe design and honestly that name is perfect because this is no longer about clicking buttons, dragging elements, and adjusting pixels one at a time. It's about direction. You tell the AI what you want. You direct it like a creative lead briefing a talented team and it executes immediately. The skill has completely changed. It's no longer in the clicking or the technical ability is in knowing what you want and being able to describe it clearly and with confidence. That's the new design skill and that is something anyone watching this video can learn and get good at quickly. Update four is instant interactive prototypes. Before this, even after you had your screens fully designed and looking great, you still had to manually link them all together. Click here goes to screen two. Tap this button goes to screen three. Swipe there goes to screen four. That process ate up a huge amount of time and required careful tedious work. Stitch now connects your screens automatically. You get a fully clickable interactive prototype without doing any of that manual linking yourself. So let's say you want to demo the AI profit boardroom member experience to someone. The signup flow, the onboarding screens, the dashboard, the community feed, the content library. You describe it in Stitch. It builds each screen. It connects them. And in minutes, you have something you can actually hand someone and let them click through. That used to take days of work. Now it takes minutes. And that kind of speed completely changes how fast you can validate an idea, pitch a new concept, test a user flow, or show a client something real before you've committed to building it properly. The last update is the most underrated one, and I think a lot of people are going to sleep on it, don't is called design.md. Think of it like a Redmi file, but for your entire design system. It stores your brand colors, your typography choices, your layout rules, your spacing guidelines, your visual tone, everything that defines how your product looks and feels in one place. And every single time you build something new in Stitch, it automatically pulls from that file. So everything you create stays consistent. Same fonts, same colors, same spacing, same vibe, every page, every screen, every component, every single time. For anyone running a brand or a product, this is enormous. The biggest weakness of AI generated design has always been inconsistency between outputs. Design.md solves that completely. You define your rules once. The AI follows them forever. You never have to correct inconsistencies again. All right, let's bring this home.
